clean_story: Poison’s breaking point.  Dose divides harm from harmless. "Toxin threshold" names the exact line where a substance shifts—from safe to dangerous, measured in milligrams per kilogram, like a hidden tripwire in your coffee or garden. Cyanide in apple seeds won’t kill you—unless you crush 200 seeds and eat them at once. That’s ~1mg/kg, the threshold where your body’s detox enzymes get overwhelmed. Botulinum toxin—the deadliest known—has a threshold of 0. 00001mg/kg. A speck smaller than a grain of salt could paralyze you. Yet in tiny doses, it smooths wrinkles as Botox. Thresholds depend on three things:—Who’s exposed (a child’s threshold is lower). —How long (arsenic in water: 0. 01mg/L over years vs. 100mg at once). —The path in (skin absorbs slower than lungs). Even water hits a threshold—drink 6 liters in an hour, and your brain swells from diluted sodium. Three related subjects are dose-response curves, hormesis effects, and LD50 tests.
